issues.title: Improve statement of what Token authorize user to do
issues.author: gloinul
issues.body:
In section 2: 

`Client: The MoQ client requesting access to media content.`

I think this statement can be improved. Because I think the authorization is to either be allowed to subscribe or publish media content.

comments.author: suhasHere
comments.body: @gloinul this should be fixed in the latest commit

issues.title: Use of MoQ Origin
issues.author: gloinul
issues.body:
In Section 2 it is stated: 

`MoQ Relay/Origin: The MoQ relay server or origin that forwards media content and requires authorization.`

So I believe this needs improvement. First is the reference into the term for what we call the authority component of the MoQ URI. I do understand that this is referred to as Origin in many context, but it is not defined in MoQT, I think relay may work here as sole term. Secondly, I stumble a bit on "requires authorization". Maybe, "and for which the endpoint has to prove it's authorization." Maybe you have an alternative that tries to be a bit crisper here?

comments.author: suhasHere
comments.body: @gloinul should be fixed in the latest commit

issues.title: Expand on matching rules, algorithm and presentation options for various MoQ Actions and Scopes
issues.author: suhasHere

issues.title: Support TokenChallenge as an Array
issues.author: suhasHere
issues.body:
Current structure:
  struct {
      TokenChallenge challenge;       // contains ONE
  token_type, issuer_name, origin_info
      uint16_t supported_token_types<1..2^16-1>;
  } MoQAuthChallenge;

@thibmeu  suggested Return an array of TokenChallenge instead.

  This is a good observation

  The current design has a limitation: since
  TokenChallenge contains a single issuer_name and
  origin_info, you can only specify one issuer and one
  scope even though you support multiple token types.

  Real scenarios where the array approach helps:

  1. Different issuers per token type:
    - Accept 0x0002 from public-issuer.example.com
  (external)
    - Accept 0x0001 from relay.example.com
  (self-issued)
  2. Different scopes per token type:
    - Bootstrap token (0x0002): Broad scope for
  connection establishment
    - Privately verifiable (0xE5AC): Narrower scope for
   specific namespaces
  3. Multiple trusted issuers for same token type:
    - Accept 0x0002 from either issuer-a.com or
  issuer-b.com

pulls.title: Improving integration with privacy pass basic tokens
pulls.author: thibmeu
pulls.body: Align diagrams with privacy pass rfcs, and improve the integration to match existing RFCs

pulls.title: Add anonymous credentials and reverse flow
pulls.author: thibmeu
pulls.body:
Specify a mechanism through which teh origin can issue it's own tokens. This is based on reverse flow privacy pass, and allow the origin to leverage privately verifiable tokens, as well as anonymous credentials.

This is to be presented at IETF 124 in Montreal. If this is the right direction, the draft could be reorganised to consider this mode as a base case.

There are some discussion to have regarding the use of `origin_info` vs `redemption_context` with anonymous credentials.

I also have not mentioned new development with [Anonymous credit token](https://samuelschlesinger.github.io/draft-act/draft-schlesinger-privacypass-act.html) as the draft is yet to be published (later today). That could be an interesting evolution. The current writting focuses on what can be deployed today.

pulls.title: No need to encode PrivateTokenAuth in base64url
pulls.author: thibmeu
pulls.body:
[AUTHORIZATION](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-moq-transport-16#section-9.3.1.5) is a key value pair with an odd value = 0x3, which means its value is a sequence of [raw bytes](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-moq-transport-16#name-key-value-pair-structure).

On the contrary to an HTTP header which has to be encoded, this is not the case here and we can use the raw value directly, which is more consistent with the byte definication of KV pair.

pulls.title: Add Privacy Pass authorization error codes
pulls.author: suhasHere

reviews.author: thibmeu
reviews.body:
having the error better specified and within a registry helps.

beyond the few nits, the main question I have is should the MoQAuthChallenge return a TokenChallenge+supported_)token_types, or simply an array with multiple token challenges? I'd be in favor of that last options as that's more flexible and aligned with existing WWW-Authenticate

comments.body: should be `0x2` per moq-transport draft

```suggestion
when the client should retry with a new token, encoded as a byte-string.
```
is byte-string safe or should we suggest base64 (rfc4648) instead?

```suggestion
               |   UNAUTHORIZED (0x2)    [        |                   |           |
```
once merged, should be open an issue against MoQ transport draft to ensure this is cross tracked and we don't end up overlapping?

```suggestion
The reverse flow, as described in {{Section 4 of PRIVACYPASS-REVERSE-FLOW}},
```

section 6 is all about using HTTP header and therefore specific deployments. section 4 is the architecture

side: i wonder if the reverse flow could not describe the various architecture in section 4 and let section 6 as "we define a new header which can be used". this way, moq+privacy pass draft fits directly

```suggestion
| 0x0106 | TOKEN_MALFORMED | {{errors}} |
| 0x0107-0x01FF | Unassigned | |
```

```suggestion
| 0x0105 | ISSUER_UNKNOWN | Token issuer is not trusted by this relay |
| 0x0106 | TOKEN_MALFORMED | Token cannot be parsed correctly |
```

```suggestion
issuance protocol. Relay MUST set at least one supported token type.
```

```suggestion
    uint16_t supported_token_types<1..2^16-1>;
```

at least one. 2 is not required

one interesting thing here is that tokenchallenge contains a token type. should we returnn an array of token challenge instead of only one token chlalenge and supported token types? this would allow to specify the supported issuers for instance, or different origin_info

```suggestion
with the `UNAUTHORIZED` (0x02) Termination Error Code defined in
```

reviews.author: suhasHere
reviews.body: Merging after accepting review comments from @thibmeu

pulls.title: Snk 7 comments
pulls.author: thibmeu
pulls.body:
made a commit instead of commenting on #7 

the main change is removing the overload of redemption_context and focusing on origin_info instead, which can be included in a presentation_context when using anonymous credential

i've also added continuous authorization via reverse flow. the previous continuous authorization section uses is (given SETUP leverages a reverse flow), but this makes it clearer to the reader

finally, there are some minor corrections on types and structure
